title: Show beginning and end of timeline dates on My Work date view
excerpt: Having the new views in My Work is great, but as a PM who manages all of my teams projects across Monday, it would be super helpful to have both the START and END date of a timeline to be shown in the date view. If I hav…

posts.author: DanielaHuber (Daniela Huber)
posts.cookedHtml: Having the new views in My Work is great, but as a PM who manages all of my teams projects across Monday, it would be super helpful to have both the START and END date of a timeline to be shown in the date view. If I have a task scheduled over several days, I only see it show up based on the END date.

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DanielaHuber - while this behavior is currently tied to monday.com’s default My Work logic, one possible workaround is to track due dates and overdue items using a timeline-based automation setup.

You might want to check out our Smart Timeline + Duration app. It gives you more control by letting you:

  • Automatically calculate and adjust timelines
  • Set reminders based on time periods or duration
  • Trigger actions when deadlines are missed

It’s not a full replacement for My Work, but it can definitely help surface important tasks using timelines and automations especially for recurring or scheduled work.

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @DanielaHuber, the Smart Timeline + Duration app comes with a 14-day free trial, so you’ll have plenty of time to explore and set up your workflow just the way you want. If you need any help along the way, our team is here for you!
